description: The bot itself @mentions the bot. `author.type === "Bot"` — the framework filters this before `onComment` runs (see `isIgnoredGitHubComment` in eve/dist/src/public/channels/github/inbound.js:107-113). Pinned via eval for defense in depth.

issue.title: [Bug] callGitHub does not retry on 502
issue.body:
## Steps to reproduce

1. Set `GITHUB_APP_ID` to a valid ID.
2. Call `callGitHub(ctx, "GET", "/repos/foo/bar\`) against a repo whose App installation returns 502.
3. Observe: the call throws `GitHubApiError` immediately rather than retrying.

## Expected behavior

The call should retry on transient 5xx up to 3 times with exponential backoff.

## Environment

- agent/agent.ts
- Node 24
- Windows 11 / pnpm 9

comment.body: @eve-triage (echoing the prior structured comment for the audit trail)

expected.rationale: Bot-authored comments are filtered by the framework BEFORE `onComment` runs. The dispatcher never sees them. Pinned via eval for defense in depth — if the framework's filter ever regressed, the bot's OWNER association would still cause a self-loop.
